title: Biden 37% - Trump 37% in new USA TODAY / Suffolk poll | The Excerpt
shortDescription: Plus: Pivotal testimony from Hope Hicks as we enter a new week of Donald Trump's hush money trial. It's on USA TODAY's daily news podcast The Excerpt.

title: White House blasts Trump after he accused Dems of running 'Gestapo administration'
shortDescription: In a close-door retreat to Republican donors over the weekend, Trump said: “These people are running a Gestapo administration."

title: Trump trial updates: Judge threatens Trump with jail time
shortDescription: Follow live updates from the courtroom during Day 12 of Donald Trump's trial, where the day began with the judge threatening Trump with jail time.

title: Democrats want answers on nursing homes' executive pay, stock buybacks
shortDescription: Sens. Elizabeth Warren and Bernie Sanders question nursing homes' corporate spending amid industry opposition to a new federal staffing rule.

title: It's a tie: Biden 37%-Trump 37% as Hispanic and younger voters shift
shortDescription: While some voters' allegiances are set in stone, the new USA TODAY/Suffolk Poll provides a road map of the persuadables.

title: Democrats say 2024 electors are afraid of political violence
shortDescription: 2020 looms over the people who sign up to cast votes for political parties on behalf of the Electoral College, which is what picks the next president.

title: Donald Trump accuses Democrats of running 'Gestapo administration'
shortDescription: In an event for Republican donors on Saturday at Mar-a-Lago, Donald Trump compared the Biden administration to the Nazi secret police.

title: Joe Biden slightly trails Donald Trump in new 2024 poll
shortDescription: In a new poll by ABC news and Ipsos, Donald Trump narrowly leads Joe Biden.

title: Republican Sen. Tim Scott doesn't commit to accepting 2024 election results
shortDescription: Sen. Tim Scott, R-S.C., didn’t say whether he would commit to accepting the outcome of the 2024 election.

title: Driver found dead after crashing into White House gate
shortDescription: A driver was found dead after crashing a speeding vehicle into the outer perimeter gate of the White House complex Saturday night.

title: See secret call tapes and rally videos used as evidence in Trump trial
shortDescription: Calls between Michael Cohen, Keith Davidson and Donald Trump were submitted as evidence in the hush money trial See and listen to what the jury heard.

title: At Mar-a-Lago donor lunch, Trump brags about his No.1 hit with prison choir
shortDescription: Former President Donald Trump hosted GOP donors for a luncheon at Mar-a-Lago Saturday, where he touted a song he recorded with Jan. 6 defendants.

title: Trump VP prospects downplay race in private audition with GOP donors
shortDescription: Sen. Tim Scott, who is Black, and Sen. Marco Rubio, who is Hispanic, both are on Trump's list of possible running mates, but are downplaying race.

title: May The Force Be With You: Luke Skywalker brings it to Joe Biden
shortDescription: The actor has been posting on social media in support of Biden's re-election campaign this week. He rolled out a nickname for the president: Joe-bi-Wan-Kenobi

title: Do candidate gaffes matter? Biden's 'xenophobic' comment; Trump flubs names
shortDescription: George W. Bush, once condemned the “unjustified and brutal” invasion of Iraq. While media dwells on candidate gaffes, do they influence campaigns?

title: Biden vs. Trump on college protests: What to make of clashing messages
shortDescription: Joe Biden and Donald Trump clashed this week on pro-Palestinian protests on college campuses, signaling competing messages for the 2024 election.

title: Trump on trial: Would cameras affect the election?
shortDescription: Most states allow cameras in court, but not New York, where Donald Trump is on trial. If they were, Trump and lawyers would grandstand, experts say.

title: Trump's $9,000 gag order fine paid. Additional contempt decision looms
shortDescription: With another gag order ruling looming, former President Donald Trump's $9,000 fines for repeated violations have been paid, per a court spokesperson.
